metadata.name: Claude Code MCP Tool Usage Evaluation
metadata.description: 10-question evaluation suite testing Claude's ability to use MCP tools effectively

question.title: Basic Coordinate Query
question.description: Test Claude's ability to query a single entity by coordinate
question.prompt: Using the MCP tools available to you, query for the entity at coordinate P0. What information does it contain?
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke the graph-query MCP tool - Must use coordinate "P0" as input - Must interpret the returned coordinate, metadata, and data fields - Should recognize P0 as the "Ground Position" (position foundation)
question.evaluation_criteria: - Tool invocation: graph-query tool was called with coordinate P0 - Correct parsing: Response includes coordinate, type, and at least one data field - Semantic understanding: Claude explains what P0 represents in the coordinate system

question.title: Natural Language Search
question.description: Test Claude's ability to translate a natural language query into a semantic search
question.prompt: Search the graph for entities related to "foundation and ground principles". Which entities are most relevant to this concept?
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke the graph-search MCP tool - Must extract semantic intent from natural language - Must choose appropriate search mode (hybrid_weighted, vector_only, etc.) - Should interpret search results with relevance scores - Should rank results by semantic relevance
question.evaluation_criteria: - Tool invocation: graph-search tool was called with semantically appropriate query - Mode selection: Chose reasonable search mode for the query - Result interpretation: Correctly identifies top-k results and explains their relevance - Score understanding: Explains what the relevance scores mean

question.title: Specification Retrieval
question.description: Test Claude's ability to retrieve detailed specifications
question.prompt: Look up the specification for "Position" coordinates. What is the structure and meaning of P0 through P5?
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke the spec-retrieve MCP tool - Must use "Position" as the title parameter - Should parse the structured specification output - Should interpret position hierarchy (P0=Ground, P1=Foundation, P2=Structure, etc.) - Should understand relationships to other coordinates
question.evaluation_criteria: - Tool invocation: spec-retrieve tool called with title "Position" - Content parsing: Correctly extracts specification content - Hierarchy understanding: Explains position levels P0-P5 correctly - Connection awareness: Identifies connections to other coordinate types

question.title: Context Gathering
question.description: Test Claude's ability to gather contextual information at different scope levels
question.prompt: Gather context around entity M2 using narrow scope (direct relationships only). Then gather context using balanced scope (2-hop relationships). What is the difference in information density?
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke graph-context tool twice with different modes - Must understand scope semantics (narrow=direct, balanced=2-hop, wide=3-hop) - Should compare and contrast the results - Should understand information density tradeoffs
question.evaluation_criteria: - Tool invocation: graph-context called with M2, narrow mode - Tool invocation: graph-context called with M2, balanced mode - Comparison: Explains differences between narrow and balanced results - Semantics: Correctly interprets what each scope level means - Analysis: Identifies when narrow vs balanced is preferable

question.title: Progressive Disclosure
question.description: Test Claude's understanding of information disclosure levels
question.prompt: Using progressive disclosure at levels 0, 3, and 5 for entity C0, explain what information is revealed at each level and why this matters.
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke graph-disclosure tool three times with different levels - Should understand disclosure levels semantically - Should interpret what fields are available at each level - Should explain the security/privacy implications
question.evaluation_criteria: - Tool invocation: graph-disclosure called with C0, level 0 - Tool invocation: graph-disclosure called with C0, level 3 - Tool invocation: graph-disclosure called with C0, level 5 - Level semantics: Correctly explains what each level reveals - Use cases: Identifies scenarios where each level is appropriate - Security awareness: Explains progressive disclosure benefits

question.title: Graph Traversal with Filtering
question.description: Test Claude's ability to navigate relationships with depth and type filtering
question.prompt: Traverse from entity S2 at depth 2, filtering for only POS0_LINKS_TO and CONTAINS relationships. What relationship patterns do you discover?
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke graph-traverse with start_coordinate, depth, and relationship_types - Should understand path semantics - Should interpret relationship type filtering - Should explain discovered patterns
question.evaluation_criteria: - Tool invocation: graph-traverse called with S2, depth 2, filtered relationship types - Path interpretation: Correctly reads and explains paths - Filter effectiveness: Explains which relationships were filtered and why - Pattern discovery: Identifies meaningful relationship patterns in results

question.title: Graph Validation and Integrity Checking
question.description: Test Claude's ability to validate graph integrity at different scopes
question.prompt: Validate the graph using "coordinates" scope to check coordinate format and uniqueness. What validation issues are found, and how would you prioritize fixing them?
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke graph-validate with scope parameter - Should interpret validation results and issue severity - Should understand what the "coordinates" scope checks - Should prioritize issues by type and impact
question.evaluation_criteria: - Tool invocation: graph-validate called with scope "coordinates" - Issue interpretation: Correctly reads validation issues - Severity assessment: Identifies critical vs warning issues - Remediation strategy: Proposes how to fix found issues

question.title: Bulk Operations: Chunking and Sync
question.description: Test Claude's ability to perform bulk operations like chunking
question.prompt: Chunk this document: "The foundation layer (P0) represents the ground principles. The structure layer (P2) builds upon the foundation. The integration layer (P5) brings coherence." Use coordinate P1 and chunk size 60. How many chunks are created and what do they contain?
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke graph-chunk with document_id, content, coordinate, and chunk_size - Should understand hierarchical chunking semantics - Should interpret chunk structure and boundaries - Should understand coordinate context inheritance
question.evaluation_criteria: - Tool invocation: graph-chunk called with all required parameters - Chunk interpretation: Correctly identifies number and content of chunks - Coordinate context: Explains how coordinate P1 is inherited - Use cases: Explains why chunking is useful for the knowledge base

parameter.name: content
parameter.value: The foundation layer (P0) represents the ground principles. The structure layer (P2) builds upon the foundation. The integration layer (P5) brings coherence.

question.title: Semantic Embedding Generation
question.description: Test Claude's understanding of embedding generation and dimensionality
question.prompt: Generate semantic embeddings for entities P0 and P5 using task type RETRIEVAL_DOCUMENT and 768-dimensional embeddings. What do embeddings represent and how might they be used?
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke graph-embed with entity_coordinates, task_type, and dimensionality - Should understand embedding purpose and semantics - Should interpret dimensionality tradeoffs - Should explain downstream use cases
question.evaluation_criteria: - Tool invocation: graph-embed called with P0 and P5 - Parameter understanding: Correctly explains task_type and dimensionality choices - Use case explanation: Describes downstream applications (search, retrieval, clustering) - Semantic awareness: Explains what embeddings encode semantically

question.title: Multi-Tool Workflow Orchestration
question.description: Test Claude's ability to orchestrate multiple tools into a coherent workflow
question.prompt: Design a workflow to answer: "What are the core principles in our knowledge base?" Use the following approach: (1) Search for "principles" entities, (2) For top 3 results, retrieve their full specifications, (3) Gather context for each using balanced scope, (4) Validate the discovered entities. Execute this workflow and synthesize findings.
question.expected_capabilities: - Must invoke graph-search for initial discovery - Must invoke spec-retrieve for top results - Must invoke graph-context for each result - Must invoke graph-validate to ensure integrity - Should orchestrate tool calls sequentially with data flow - Should synthesize results from multiple tools
question.evaluation_criteria: - Workflow orchestration: Correctly chains tool calls with appropriate dependencies - Data flow: Passes results from one tool to next (e.g., search results to spec-retrieve) - Tool selection: Each tool was appropriate for its step in the workflow - Synthesis: Combines results into coherent answer about principles - Error handling: Handles any tool failures gracefully - Efficiency: Minimizes unnecessary tool calls

tool.name: spec-retrieve
tool.description: Called for each top search result

metric.name: Tool Invocation Accuracy
metric.description: Percentage of required MCP tools correctly invoked with appropriate parameters

metric.name: Parameter Understanding
metric.description: Correctness of parameter values and semantic appropriateness for the query context

metric.name: Result Interpretation
metric.description: Accuracy and depth of interpreting tool outputs, including nested data structures

metric.name: Semantic Reasoning
metric.description: Quality of reasoning about coordinate semantics, relationship types, and graph structure

metric.name: Workflow Execution
metric.description: For multi-tool questions: correctness of orchestration and data flow between tools

grading_rubric.score_range: Excellent - Claude demonstrates mastery of MCP tool usage
Good - Claude effectively uses MCP tools with minor issues
Acceptable - Claude understands tool usage but has some gaps
Poor - Claude struggles with tool selection or parameter usage
Failing - Claude cannot effectively use MCP tools

prerequisites.requirement: Neo4j container must be running (docker-compose up neo4j)
MCP server must be configured and running
GEMINI_API_KEY optional but recommended for embedding tests (Q9)
Claude Code must have access to MCP tools

execution_instructions.step: Setup Neo4j and MCP server using docker-compose
Initialize Claude Code with MCP tool access
For each question (Q1-Q10), provide the prompt to Claude
Observe which MCP tools Claude invokes and with what parameters
Score each question based on rubric
Aggregate scores and determine overall pass/fail

known_limitations.limitation: Q9 (embeddings) may fail or skip if GEMINI_API_KEY not configured
Some queries may timeout on very large graphs - adjust expectations for scale
Claude's tool invocation depends on system prompt configuration
Results vary based on Neo4j data population state

guide_item.explanation: Six coordinate types (C, P, M, S, T, L) form a 6-dimensional address space. P0=Ground, P5=Integration. M2 often represents "paradigm/graph layer".
hybrid_weighted (default): balances semantic and graph similarity. vector_only: pure semantic. graph_only: pure structural. hybrid_rrf: reciprocal rank fusion.
narrow=direct relationships, balanced=2-hop, wide=3-hop. Increasing scope increases information density and query time.
